description: Official pilot benchmark summary for ORI-C generalization assessment. Version 2.0: all pilots decidable after full pipeline on densified data.

A.note: Canonical datasets (synthetic, FRED, validation protocol) are tracked in dual proof manifest, not in pilot benchmark.

rejection_causes.sector_bio.pilot_pbdb_marine: C1 failed: no transition detected (det_rate=0.0). 45/45 decidable. True negative.
rejection_causes.sector_ai_tech.pilot_llm_scaling: C1 failed: no transition detected (det_rate=0.0, sigma_zero_post=1.0). 45/45 decidable. Synthetic-calibrated data.

primary.rationale: Longest pilot (500 segments), strongest detection, outside socio-economic domain, 5 class labels

secondary.rationale: Market dynamics, clear regime transition, demonstrates cross-domain portability

exploitable_positives: COVID excess mortality (health, 192 pts, ACCEPT, borderline power)
EEG Bonn (neuro, 500 pts, ACCEPT, adequate power)
Pantheon SN (cosmo, 150 pts, ACCEPT, borderline power) — upgraded from C
confirmed_negatives: PBDB marine (bio, 140 pts, REJECT, borderline power) — no transition detected
LLM scaling (ai_tech, 120 pts, REJECT, borderline power) — no transition detected
prudent_reading.note: All 7 pilots are decidable at Level B. 5 ACCEPT across 5 domains. 2 REJECT demonstrate framework specificity. Zero indeterminate verdicts remain.

upgrade_completed.note: All 3 former Level C pilots completed the full ORI-C validation pipeline (C1+C2+C3, window sensitivity, subsample stability). All produced decidable verdicts with 0 indeterminate runs.
